title: bare_address: < inside comment body extracts wrong addr
description: TRIGGER: Header value 'user@example.com (comment < with > inside it)' passed to bare_address(). BAD BEHAVIOR: rfind('<') matches the '<' inside the RFC 5322 comment body; addr[start..].find('>') then matches the '>' inside the comment, returning the junk string between them (e.g., ' with ') instead of 'user@example.com'. The function should skip '<' characters inside '(...)' comments before deciding which '<' is the angle-bracket delimiter. Address test on such a header returns a wrong match result.
